verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, Deus salutis meæ,  in die clamavi et nocte coram te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְ֭הֹוָה אֱלֹהֵ֣י יְשׁוּעָתִ֑י יוֹם־צָעַ֖קְתִּי בַלַּ֣יְלָה נֶגְדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: O Lord God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: Intret in conspectu tuo oratio mea,  inclina aurem tuam ad precem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תָּב֣וֹא לְ֭פָנֶיךָ תְּפִלָּתִ֑י הַטֵּ֥ה אׇ֝זְנְךָ֗ לְרִנָּתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Let my prayer come before thee: incline thine ear unto my cry;

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia repleta est malis anima mea,  et vita mea inferno appropinquavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־שָׂבְעָ֣ה בְרָע֣וֹת נַפְשִׁ֑י וְ֝חַיַּ֗י לִשְׁא֥וֹל הִגִּֽיעוּ׃
verses.king_james: For my soul is full of troubles: and my life draweth nigh unto the grave.

verses.latin_vulgate: Æstimatus sum cum descendentibus in lacum,  factus sum sicut homo sine adjutorio,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶ֭חְשַׁבְתִּי עִם־י֣וֹרְדֵי ב֑וֹר הָ֝יִ֗יתִי כְּגֶ֣בֶר אֵֽין־אֱיָֽל׃
verses.king_james: I am counted with them that go down into the pit: I am as a man that hath no strength:

verses.latin_vulgate: inter mortuos liber ;  sicut vulnerati dormientes in sepulchris,  quorum non es memor amplius,  et ipsi de manu tua repulsi sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּמֵּתִ֗ים חׇ֫פְשִׁ֥י כְּמ֤וֹ חֲלָלִ֨ים ׀ שֹׁ֥כְבֵי קֶ֗בֶר אֲשֶׁ֤ר לֹ֣א זְכַרְתָּ֣ם ע֑וֹד וְ֝הֵ֗מָּה מִיָּדְךָ֥ נִגְזָֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: Free among the dead, like the slain that lie in the grave, whom thou rememberest no more: and they are cut off from thy hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuerunt me in lacu inferiori,  in tenebrosis, et in umbra mortis.
verses.king_james: Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the deeps.

verses.latin_vulgate: Super me confirmatus est furor tuus,  et omnes fluctus tuos induxisti super me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָ֭לַי סָמְכָ֣ה חֲמָתֶ֑ךָ וְכׇל־מִ֝שְׁבָּרֶ֗יךָ עִנִּ֥יתָ סֶּֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: Thy wrath lieth hard upon me, and thou hast afflicted me with all thy waves. Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Longe fecisti notos meos a me ;  posuerunt me abominationem sibi.  Traditus sum, et non egrediebar ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִרְחַ֥קְתָּ מְיֻדָּעַ֗י מִ֫מֶּ֥נִּי שַׁתַּ֣נִי תוֹעֵב֣וֹת לָ֑מוֹ כָּ֝לֻ֗א וְלֹ֣א אֵצֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: I am shut up, and I cannot come forth.

verses.latin_vulgate: oculi mei languerunt præ inopia.  Clamavi ad te, Domine, tota die ;  expandi ad te manus meas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵינִ֥י דָאֲבָ֗ה מִנִּ֫י־עֹ֥נִי קְרָאתִ֣יךָ יְהֹוָ֣ה בְּכׇל־י֑וֹם שִׁטַּ֖חְתִּי אֵלֶ֣יךָ כַפָּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Mine eye mourneth by reason of affliction: Lord, I have called daily upon thee, I have stretched out my hands unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid mortuis facies mirabilia ?  aut medici suscitabunt, et confitebuntur tibi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלַמֵּתִ֥ים תַּעֲשֶׂה־פֶּ֑לֶא אִם־רְ֝פָאִ֗ים יָק֤וּמוּ ׀ יוֹד֬וּךָ סֶּֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: Wilt thou shew wonders to the dead? shall the dead arise and praise thee? Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid narrabit aliquis in sepulchro misericordiam tuam,  et veritatem tuam in perditione ?
verses.king_james: Shall thy lovingkindness be declared in the grave? or thy faithfulness in destruction?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid cognoscentur in tenebris mirabilia tua ?  et justitia tua in terra oblivionis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיִוָּדַ֣ע בַּחֹ֣שֶׁךְ פִּלְאֶ֑ךָ וְ֝צִדְקָתְךָ֗ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ נְשִׁיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Shall thy wonders be known in the dark? and thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego ad te, Domine, clamavi,  et mane oratio mea præveniet te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֤י ׀ אֵלֶ֣יךָ יְהֹוָ֣ה שִׁוַּ֑עְתִּי וּ֝בַבֹּ֗קֶר תְּֽפִלָּתִ֥י תְקַדְּמֶֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: But unto thee have I cried, O Lord; and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut quid, Domine, repellis orationem meam ;  avertis faciem tuam a me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָמָ֣ה יְ֭הֹוָה תִּזְנַ֣ח נַפְשִׁ֑י תַּסְתִּ֖יר פָּנֶ֣יךָ מִמֶּֽנִּי׃
verses.king_james: Lord, why castest thou off my soul? why hidest thou thy face from me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Pauper sum ego, et in laboribus a juventute mea ;  exaltatus autem, humiliatus sum et conturbatus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָ֘נִ֤י אֲנִ֣י וְגֹוֵ֣עַ מִנֹּ֑עַר נָשָׂ֖אתִי אֵמֶ֣יךָ אָפֽוּנָה׃
verses.king_james: I am afflicted and ready to die from my youth up: while I suffer thy terrors I am distracted.

verses.latin_vulgate: In me transierunt iræ tuæ,  et terrores tui conturbaverunt me :
verses.king_james: Thy fierce wrath goeth over me; thy terrors have cut me off.

verses.latin_vulgate: circumdederunt me sicut aqua tota die ;  circumdederunt me simul.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סַבּ֣וּנִי כַ֭מַּיִם כׇּל־הַיּ֑וֹם הִקִּ֖יפוּ עָלַ֣י יָֽחַד׃
verses.king_james: They came round about me daily like water; they compassed me about together.

verses.latin_vulgate: Elongasti a me amicum et proximum,  et notos meos a miseria.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִרְחַ֣קְתָּ מִ֭מֶּנִּי אֹהֵ֣ב וָרֵ֑עַ מְֽיֻדָּעַ֥י מַחְשָֽׁךְ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Lover and friend hast thou put far from me, and mine acquaintance into darkness.
